texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Olefins/paraffins separation is one of the most important tasks in industries, especially in petrochemical industries, which is traditionally performed by different processes such as distillation (low temperature or extractive distillation), chemical and physical sorption and membrane processes (Faiz and Li, 2012). Distillation is a common, energy extensive and expensive fractionation process, e. g. it's united states' annual energy consumption was reported by Kunesh et al. as 2.87 × 10 18 J (Kunesh et al., 1995); thereby, researchers try to replace this process by the other more proper and lower energy demand processes. Absorption process via 𝛑 -complexation is an economic process due to it's high selectivity and low energy consumption. Despite these advantages, the traditional form of chemical absorption column has some shortcomings such as foaming, flooding, channeling, etc (Faiz et al., 2012; Faiz and Li, 2012). Nevertheless, this process is not economical for large scale separation processes (Klaassen et al., 2005).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Removal of olefin gaseous compounds such as ethylene, propylene, 1-butene and butadiene from paraffins is one of the most crucial issues in the petrochemical industries. The supported liquid membrane of silver nitrate solution supported in PVDF microfilter membrane was used for 1-butene/isobutane separation in the current study. The effects of silver nitrate concentration, trans-membrane pressure and gaseous feed stream's humidity were investigated on the membrane separation performance. According to the results, as the salt concentration increased from 0 to 3 M, 1-butene permeance and 1-butene/isobutane selectivity were increased from 1.96 × 10 -5 to 0.0607 gpu and 0.0004 to 1380, respectively. In the liquid membrane containing aqueous solution of 1 M silver nitrate, 1-butene/isobutane selectivity were found as 100 and 330 for the dry and humidified feed gas streams. Cu(NO3)2.3H2O salt was used simultaneously with the silver salt in the aqueous liquid membrane in order to increase the long-term stability of the membrane in addition to the feed stream humidification. As the both salts were used simultaneously in 1 M concentration, 1-butene/isobutane selectivity decreased slightly from 439 to 399, while the 1-butene permeance increased from 0.0227 to 0.075 gpu, during 4 h continuous operation.

texts.orig: Koros and Staudt-Bickel studies showed that the polyimide membranes have low olefins/paraffins selectivities and accordingly they are not useful for industrial applications (Nymeijer et al., 2004; StaudtBickel and Koros, 2000). Beside the advantages of high olefins/paraffins selectivities and performance stabilities (Pan et al., 2012), inorganic membranes have some disadvantages such as the hard and expensive preparation processes, low modular membrane surface area and complexity of the regeneration cycles. Therefore their applications are limited in the olefins/paraffins gas separation tasks (Marcos Fallanza Torices and Daniel Gorri Cirella, 2013).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The facilitated transport membranes usually have high olefins permeances and selectivities in olefins/paraffins separation, are environmentally friendly, require low space and capital cost and low energy consumption and also have fewer industrial restrictions than the other membrane processes (Azhin et al., 2008; Takht Ravanchi et al., 2009). These membrane processes can be divided into three categories of supported or immobilized liquid membranes (SLM or ILM), solvent swollen fixed site carrier membranes and solid polymer electrolyte membranes. The supported liquid membranes among them, are more common in use (Ravanchi et al., 2010a). The electrolyte membranes industrial usage is limited yet due to the impermanence of silver ions as they converted into silver nanoparticles and on the other hand they cannot be regenerated.
texts.text: The facilitated transport membranes usually have high olefins permeances and selectivities in olefins/paraffins separation, are environmentally friendly, require low space and capital cost and low energy consumption and also have fewer industrial restrictions than the other membrane processes (Azhin et al., 2008; Takht Ravanchi et al., 2009). These membrane processes can be divided into three categories of supported or immobilized liquid membranes (SLM or ILM), solvent swollen fixed site carrier membranes and solid polymer electrolyte membranes. The supported liquid membranes among them, are more common in use (Ravanchi et al., 2010a). The electrolyte membranes industrial usage is limited yet due to the impermanence of silver ions as they converted into silver nanoparticles and on the other hand they cannot be regenerated.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Supported liquids in porous membranes that include operating carriers, are promising solutions. Carrier solution are kept in the pores of the porous membrane by capillary forces (Faiz and Li, 2012). Penetrating species, e.g. olefins here, form some complexes with the membrane's carrier agents and permeate from the higher pressure side of the membrane into the low pressure side and the complexes are broken there to release olefins to the permeate side (Ravanchi et al., 2010a). Although these membranes have high selectivities for olefins/paraffins separation, however they have been showed little mechanical stabilities due to the carrier liquid movement out of the membrane's pores as the feed pressure exceed than a critical value. Additionally, these membrane types have acceptable separation performances in moisturized environments (Ravanchi et al., 2010b).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Kovvali et al. used polyvinilydene diflouride (PVDF) supported ILM with 7.5 M silver nitrate-glycerol for 1-butene/ n -butane separation. 1butene permeability and 1-butene/ n -butane selectivity were reported as 260 Barrer and 850, respectively. They reported acceptable long-term stability in the membrane's separation performance (Kovvali et al., 2001). Shukla et al. used nafion membrane via AgNO3 solution for 1-butene/ n -butane separation. According to their results, 1-butene permeability and 1-butene/ n -butane ideal selectivity were 2.9 × 10 -6 cm 3 (STP)/cm 2 scm Hg and 1000, respectively (Nymeijer et al., 2004). Eriksen et al. studied ethane/ethylene separation performance using AgBF4 solution supported in nafion membrane with the humidified feed stream. They found the ethane permeability and ethane/ethylene ideal separation factor as 2688 Barrer and 1934, respectively (Eriksen et al., 1993a, 1993b). Ravanchi et al. used AgNO3 solution supported in PVDF membrane for separation of propylene/propane mixture gas and found 250 for propylene/propane selectivity with the humidified feed stream (Ravanchi et al., 2010a, 2010b). Duan et al. developed a double layer liquid membrane consist of hydrophobic PVDF and hydrophilic PTFE (poly(tetrafluoro ethylene)) membranes with nonvolatile facilitation solvent of triethylene glycol (TEG) containing different silver salts concentration for propane/propylene separation. According to their results, propylene selectivities were found as 2.4, 40 and 100 for TEG, TEG/AgNO3 and TEG/AgBF4, respectively (Duan et al., 2003). R. Surya Murali et al. used 0-50 Wt. % silver tetra fluoro borate (AgBF4) incorporated pebax-1657 (poly(ether-block-amide)) membranes for separation of three different binary mixtures of propylene-propane (50/50, 66/34, 90/10) at the pressure range of 2-6 bar. As AgBF4 loading increases in the pebax membrane from 0 to 50 Wt.%, propylene-propane selectivity increased from 2.11 to 20.38 for the 50/50 propylene-propane feed gas stream (Surya Murali et al., 2015). Adachi et al. investigated n -butane, 1butene and 1,3-butadiene permeations through an hydrated Ag + -doped perfluorocarbon-type ion-exchange membranes. The ideal separation factor of l-butene/ n -butane was reported as 5400 in the membranes as Na + exchanged by Ag + using AgNO3 aqueous solution (Adachi et al., 1998).

texts.orig: In the current study, some supported liquid in PVDF membranes were prepared using silver nitrate as carrier and employed for the selective separation of 1-butene/isobutane gaseous mixture. Outstanding notes of the current study is to employ lower concentrations of complexing agents for real industrial grad gas streams where humidified also and relatively stable performance were observed as discussed in the results and discussion section. Relatively lower studies on the C4 olefins were found in the literature where focused in the current study.

texts.orig: This gas separation task was risen in Natural Gas Liquids (NGL) Fractionation Unit of Iranian petrochemical plants where their exiting gas stream contains 2000-3000 ppm olefins (mainly C4 olefins). Accompanying of these olefin compounds results in some difficulties in their downstream plants such as Methyl Tertiary Butyl Ether (MTBE) production plant. The experiments procedure and their results are discussed in the following sections.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Industrial grades of 1-butene from Amir Kabir petrochemical complex and isobutane and nitrogen from Bandar-Imam petrochemical complexes were dedicated and used as received. 1-butene and isobutane were employed as the feed gas streams and nitrogen was used as the sweep gas. The gaseous 1-butene and isobutene analysis are presented in Tables 1 and 2, respectively. The PVDF flat sheet membranes (4.7 cm in diameter, 30 𝛍 m in thickness and effective area of 14.51 cm 2 ) were purchased from Navigator Co., China, and used as the SLMs' supports. Silver nitrate (AgNO3, GR Pro Analysis) and copper(II) nitrate (Cu(NO3)2.3H2O GR Pro Analysis) were purchased from Merck Co. and used as the carrier as received.

The gaseous 1-butene and isobutene analysis are presented in Tables 1 and 2, respectively. texts.label: text
texts.orig: The employed membrane module and it's screws in the current study were made from stainless steel (AISI 316). The membrane module has two separate parts where the membrane is placed between them. In order to prevent gas leakage between metal parts, one rubbery O-ring was implemented in the lower part of the membrane module. A schematic diagram of the membrane module is shown in Fig. 1. The module valves and all fittings were made from stainless steel (AISI 316). A gas washing bottle with capacity of 400 cm 3 water was used for humidification of the feed gas stream. Process flow diagram of the employed setup is shown in Fig. 2.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After sealing the experimental setup and membrane module in the feed and the permeate sides, isobutane or 1-butene feed streams were passed through mass flow meters and then entered the humidifier (gas washing bottle) and finally the feed stream entered into the membrane module. In some prescribed experiments the feed stream straightly enters the membrane module without humidification in order to investigate impact of the feed stream humidity on the membrane separation performance. The retentate and permeate streams leave the membrane module through their designed outlet ports. Dry nitrogen was used as sweep gas for the permeate stream at pressure of 1 atm during the all experiments. All industrial grade feed and permeate samples were analyzed using GC.

texts.orig: 1-butene passes through the membrane by two mechanisms: Fickian diffusion and reversible complex formation with silver ions. Fickian diffusion law expresses that a components concentration gradient across the membrane causes mass transfer of the component. As shown in Fig. 4, in the absence of carrier in the liquid membrane, 1-butene penetrates only by the Fickian diffusion, while as the silver salt added into the membrane solution and it's concentration increases, permeance of 1-butene increasesalso due to it's 𝛑 -complex formation potential with the silver ions. The complex formation facilitates 1-butene transport across the liquid membrane while as it can be seen in Fig. 3, by addition of silver salt into the liquid membrane, permeance of isobutane decreases due to the isobutane molecules' disability to form 𝛑 -complexes. This leads to restriction of their penetration

texts.orig: The 1-butene/isobutane selectivities for different membranes are shown in Fig. 5. As it can be seen, the liquid membrane's 1-butene/isobutane selectivities are low in the absence of silver nitrate, e.g. the water as a liquid membrane is approximately non-selective. As the silver salt dissolves in solvent of liquid membrane, i.e. water, up to 3 M, the isobutane permeance decreases continuously while the 1-butene/isobutane selectivity is increased. This can be attributed to the complex formation ability of silver cations (Ravanchi et al., 2010b).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Gas permeation rate through the membrane occurs by two mechanisms of selective and nonselective permeation. As long as the support membrane pores are filled by the liquid membrane, the dominant permeation mechanism is the selective permeation by the 𝛑 -complex formation with the carrier to selectively pass 1-butene molecules. As the liquid membrane's solvent (i.e. water) evaporates during the process and some the support's pores are dried, the nonor less selective permeation starts till it becomes the dominant transport mechanism through the membrane which causes increasing the permeation rate of both gases (Figs. 3 and 4 for dry feed stream) and decreasing the overall membrane's 1-butene/isobutane selectivity (Fig. 5 for dry feed stream) (Teramoto et al., 1986). It seems the feed gas humidification aids keeping the liquid membrane water content and also the 𝛑 -complex forming agent(s) mobility (Ravanchi, Kaghazchi et al. 2010).

texts.orig: From Fig. 4, it can also be concluded that as the dry feed gas stream is introduced into the membrane cell, the water content of the liquid membrane reduces due to it's evaporation into the dry feed gas stream. This evaporation causes increasing the concentration of the carrier and also reducing the thickness of the membrane during the process. Each of the mentioned phenomenon has same effects on the 1-butene permeation (i.e. reducing the thickness of the membrane and increasing the concentration of the carrier causes increasing of 1-butene permeation).

texts.orig: Cu(NO3)2 slat has low network formation energy and is rapid water soluble, then it is a good choice for among a variety of copper salts. The formed olefin complex copper cations are more stable than those formed with silver cations; however separation performance of copper ions seems to be weaker than that of silver cations for olefin/paraffin separation. Presence of Cu 2+ cations in liquid membrane along with Ag + cations in the electrolyte membrane structure were employed as a solution to postpone restoring of the silver ions as the main carriers in the membrane (Esmaeili, 2013). In the current study it was also tried to use these cations simultaneously in the liquid membrane.

Employing alone the silver nitrate salt in the liquid membrane, it's separation performance deteriorates over the time (e.g. isobutane permeance increases, Fig. 8). It is not acceptable and promising for scaling up the membrane process to the larger scales. In order to overcome this deficiency, Cu(NO3)2 slat was also used with the silver nitrate salt to keep the membrane separation performance over the time, in addition to humidification of the feed gas stream. texts.orig: 4. Conclusion
texts.text: 4. Conclusion

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Removal of gaseous olefin compounds such as ethylene, propylene, 1-butene and butadiene from paraffin streams is one of the crucial issues in the petrochemical industries. In the current study, some silver nitrate aqueous solutions supported in PVDF membranes were employed for 1-butene/isobutane separation. The effects of silver nitrate concentration, transmembrane pressure and humidity of the feed gas stream on the membrane separation performance were investigated. According to the results, as the salt concentration increased from 0 to 3 M, 1-butene and isobutane permeance increased and decreased, respectively. In salt concentration of 3 M, 1butene permeance and it's selectivity over isobutane was measured as 0.0247 gpu and 1380, respectively. The 1 M silver nitrate concentration aqueous solution as liquid membrane showed higher selectivity of 1-butene/isobutane of 330 for the humidified feed gas stream and 250 for dry feed gas stream. As the trans-membrane increases from 0.2 to 1 bar, 1-butenepermeance and 1-butene/isobutane selectivity were increased from 0.0215 gpu and 326 to 0.0458 gpu and 585, respectively.
texts.text: Removal of gaseous olefin compounds such as ethylene, propylene, 1-butene and butadiene from paraffin streams is one of the crucial issues in the petrochemical industries. In the current study, some silver nitrate aqueous solutions supported in PVDF membranes were employed for 1-butene/isobutane separation. The effects of silver nitrate concentration, transmembrane pressure and humidity of the feed gas stream on the membrane separation performance were investigated. According to the results, as the salt concentration increased from 0 to 3 M, 1-butene and isobutane permeance increased and decreased, respectively. In salt concentration of 3 M, 1butene permeance and it's selectivity over isobutane was measured as 0.0247 gpu and 1380, respectively. The 1 M silver nitrate concentration aqueous solution as liquid membrane showed higher selectivity of 1-butene/isobutane of 330 for the humidified feed gas stream and 250 for dry feed gas stream. As the trans-membrane increases from 0.2 to 1 bar, 1-butenepermeance and 1-butene/isobutane selectivity were increased from 0.0215 gpu and 326 to 0.0458 gpu and 585, respectively.

The second salt addition (1 M AgNO3:Cu(NO3)2) solution and simultaneously humidification of the feed gas stream, resulted in the keeping the membrane separation performance nearly constant over the time as it's 1-butene/isobutane selectivity was slightly decreased from 439 to 399 after 4 h. The separation performance of the prepared membrane was compared with the other published separation performance result and showed acceptable values while relatively lower concentration of silver salts were used. It should be said that much more consideration should be considered for this scale and the larger scale of industrial applications for this membranes.
